body:
## Summary

A reference markdown test document has been created to validate Google Docs capture fidelity. It covers 14 markdown feature categories and is designed to survive DOCX round-trip conversion. This document should be used to validate and improve both `--capture api` and `--capture browser` modes.

## Public test document

**Google Docs URL:** https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit

All URL variations should be supported:
- `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>`
- `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit`
- `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit?usp=sharing`
- `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=...&rtpof=true&sd=true`
- `https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOC_ID>/edit?tab=t.0`

## Reference files

- [Gist with source markdown and all captured outputs](https://gist.github.com/konard/f9533797242666c03097440fd3e30ad5)
  - `markdown-test-document.md` — source (ground truth)
  - `captured-rust-api.md` — Rust v0.3.1, `--capture api`
  - `captured-js-api.md` — JS v1.7.8, `--capture api`
  - `captured-js-browser.md` — JS v1.7.8, `--capture browser`

The DOCX file and 4 PNG test images are in the [web-capture-test working directory](https://gist.github.com/konard/f9533797242666c03097440fd3e30ad5).

## Features tested (14 sections)

| # | Feature | # | Feature |
|---|---------|---|---------|
| 1 | Headings (H1–H6) | 8 | Tables (simple, aligned, formatted) |
| 2 | Inline formatting (bold, italic, strikethrough) | 9 | Links |
| 3 | Paragraphs | 10 | Images (4 embedded PNGs) |
| 4 | Blockquotes | 11 | Horizontal rules |
| 5 | Unordered lists (nested 3 levels) | 12 | Special characters & emoji |
| 6 | Ordered lists (nested 3 levels) | 13 | Nested formatting edge cases |
| 7 | Mixed lists (ordered + unordered) | 14 | Empty/minimal table cells |

## Current capture results

### `--capture api` (both Rust and JS)

Both use `/export?format=html`. Major issues found:

| Feature | Status | Issue |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Headings | **Broken** | Rust: H1/H2 become `=====` underline style with empty `[]()` anchors prepended. JS: heading numbers escaped (`1\.`) |
| Bold/italic/strikethrough | **Lost** | All inline formatting stripped in both versions |
| Blockquotes | **Lost** | `>` prefix completely missing, rendered as plain paragraphs |
| Nested lists | **Degraded** | Nesting partially lost or flattened |
| Tables | **Partially broken** | Rust: mostly preserved. JS: mostly preserved but some formatting lost |
| Images | **Preserved** | 4/4 images extracted in both versions |
| Links | **Degraded** | Wrapped in Google redirect URLs |
| ` ` entities | **Not decoded** | Em dash lines show raw ` ` |

### `--capture browser` (JS only, Rust is a no-op per #80)

JS v1.7.8 navigates to `/edit` but Google Docs renders via canvas — no DOM content. Result:

| Feature | Status | Issue |
|---------|--------|-------|
| **All formatting** | **Lost** | Bold, italic, strikethrough, headings — all plain text |
| **All images** | **Lost** | 0/4 images captured (shown as `*` placeholder) |
| **All links** | **Lost** | URLs completely missing |
| **Tables** | **Broken** | Multi-column tables collapsed to single-column |
| **Lists** | **Lost** | All list structure removed, items as plain paragraphs |
| **Blockquotes** | **Lost** | Plain paragraphs |
| **Horizontal rules** | **Broken** | Rendered as single `-` character |

## What needs to happen

1. **`--capture browser` should use `DOCS_modelChunk` interception** (as described in #72) to extract the full document model from the `/edit` page, which contains all text, formatting, images, and structure
2. **`--capture api` should improve HTML-to-markdown conversion** to preserve inline formatting, blockquotes, and heading styles from the export HTML
3. **This test document should be added to automated tests** — capture the public Google Doc at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit and compare output against the reference `markdown-test-document.md`
4. **All Google Docs URL variations** listed above should be accepted and handled correctly

## Related issues

- #72 — Google Docs: support all capture methods (public export, Docs API, browser DOM)
- #80 — Rust: `--capture browser` does not launch a browser
- #81 — JS: `--capture browser` drops all images and content

## Applies to

- [x] Rust CLI
- [x] JavaScript CLI

Example md and docs archive: [markdown-test-document.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26899042/markdown-test-document.zip)

We need to use https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM (that is test public document, that is created exactly for that purpose) in our CI/CD tests, and make sure both JavaScript and Rust support real browser capture with no fallbacks, when `--capture browser` is used.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.
title: Add markdown round-trip test document for Google Docs capture validation
